---
title: "Conference"
---

**Date:** Sept 30-Oct 2nd, 2026\
**Location:** Tilburg University, The Netherlands\
**Venue:** Simon Building, Prof. Cobbenhagenlaan 225\
**Registration:** <https://edu.nl/guntn>
<!-- **Submission page:** <https://edu.nl/mj9x6> -->

Tilburg University will host the first annual meeting of the Theory Methods Society. The conference will last two days, with an optional social program and a preconference workshop.

![](campus.jpg)


## Preliminary Program

**Note: The program is still preliminary and subject to change.**

```{r}
dt_wrap <- function(dat, childrow, options = NULL){
  colnum <- which(names(dat) == childrow)
  datatable(
    cbind(' ' = '<img src="https://datatables.net/examples/resources/details_open.png"/>', dat), escape = -1,
    options = c(list(
      columnDefs = list(
        list(visible = FALSE, targets = colnum),
        list(orderable = FALSE, className = 'details-control', targets = 0)
      )
    ), options),
    callback = JS(paste0("table.column(0).nodes().to$().css({cursor: 'pointer'});
  var format = function(d) {
    return '<div style=\"background-color:#eee; padding: .5em;\"> ' +
            d[", colnum, "] + '</div>';
  };
  table.on('click', 'td.details-control', function() {
    var td = $(this), row = table.row(td.closest('tr'));
    if (row.child.isShown()) {
      row.child.hide();
      td.html('<img src=\"https://datatables.net/examples/resources/details_open.png\"/>');
    } else {
      row.child(format(row.data())).show();
      td.html('<img src=\"https://datatables.net/examples/resources/details_close.png\"/>');
    }
  });")
    ), rownames = FALSE)
}
sched <- read.csv("conference_program.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
row.names(sched) <- NULL
sched$Description[is.na(sched$Description)] <- "Not (yet) available"
sched <- sched[ , c("Start", "Duration", "Type", "Speaker", "Title", "Description")]
names(sched)[1:2] <- c("Start", "Mins")
library(DT)
dt_wrap(sched, "Description", options = list(dom = 't', "pageLength" = nrow(sched)))
```

## Important Dates

```{r}
tab <- c("Submissions Open", "20-02-2026",
"Submission Deadline", "18-06-2026",
"Registration opens", "18-06-2026",
"Decision Notifications", "rolling",
"Program announced", "rolling",
"Registration Deadline", "17-08-2026",
"Preconference Workshop", "30-09-2026",
"Conference day 1", "01-10-2026",
"Conference day 2", "02-10-2026")

tab <- matrix(tab, ncol = 2, byrow = T)
tab <- data.frame(tab)
# tab$X2 <- as.Date(tab$X2, format = "%d-%m-%Y")
# library(lubridate)
# tab$X2 <- ymd(tab$X2)
# #tab$X2[1:5] %m+% months(1) -> tab$X2[1:5]
names(tab) <- c("Milestone", "Date")
knitr::kable(tab)
```


## Preconference Workshop

This workshop is organized in collaboration with the eScience Center and the Open Science Community Tilburg.

Teachers: MSc [Rasoul Norouzi](https://www.tilburguniversity.edu/staff/r-norouzinikjeh) (Tilburg University), dr. [Erik Tjong Kim Sang](https://www.esciencecenter.nl/team/dr-ir-erik-tjong-kim-sang/) (eScience Center)

For our preconference workshop, we partnered with the eScience Center NL. Thanks to their expertise, you can now learn four main skills:

1. Automatically downloading published papers in bulk for systematic review, text mining, bibliometric analysis, etc., using the COSMAS tool developed by the eScience Center
1. Constructing theories based on causal claims in published papers, downloaded using the COSMAS tool
1. Harmonizing social science concepts (e.g., recognizing that "sleep disorder" and "insomnia" refer to the same concept)
1. Knowledge extraction of other relevant information from published papers using specialized models with known validity and reliability (so, not ChatGPT) - like p-values, sample sizes, etc.



## Submission formats

### Keynotes

An invited talk of approximately 40 minutes, followed by 20 minutes of discussion.

<!-- ### Keynotes -->

<!-- An invited format, where participants work on an interactive activity in smaller groups, with the aim to bring multiple perspectives to bear on an urgent issue in theory (methods). -->

### Submitted Talks

This format is a 20 minute talk, plus 10 minute discussion or Q&A. Anyone can submit a talk like this, and multiple presenters can share the stage to present a join project or discuss opposing views. Optionally, indicate whether your submitted talk should be considered for a Lightning Talk or Poster slot, if it is not selected for a Submitted Talk.

### Lightning Talks

This format is a 5 minute elevator pitch designed to inspire and spark discussion. Speakers can present a paper, idea, software, or other resource.

### Poster

There is a 1.5 hour slot for poster presentations, during which there is a walking lunch in the same room to stimulate conversations.

## Participation Costs

*Expected: 100-140 euros*

This covers participation in all sessions, catering during the conference days, and participation in the conference dinner.

We want to be inclusive, so the registration form will include an option to apply for a registration fee waiver if you do not have a conference budget, optionally in exchange for volunteering at the conference.

Waivers will be awarded subject to our financial position and the number of applicants.

<!-- ## Registration -->

<!-- You can apply for the summer school via *link to be anounced*. -->

<!-- We can accommodate a maximum of 40 participants. Please note that participants will be selected on a first-come, first-served basis, and registration is only confirmed after you have received an email from us. We maintain a waiting list for participants who sign up beyond the first 40. -->
